tossups.question: A woman in this novel writes a letter declaring, “I wish I weren’t as strong as a horse,” and observing, “I’ve caught belief like a disease. I’ve fallen into belief like love.” In this novel, a woman’s diary entries compare married life to a “desert” and describe a dream of ascending a flooded staircase. Paralleling a scene in a film that he wrote, a man in this novel falls in love with a woman when she eats some (*) onions, even though her husband hates their smell. This novel’s narrator calls it “a record of hate far more than of love.” After a woman in this novel dies from pneumonia, the liver-spots on the cheek of the militant atheist Smythe (“smith”) are healed. This novel’s title event occurs when a woman makes a desperate deal with God after her lover is nearly killed by a V-1 rocket during the Blitz. For 10 points, name this novel about the relationship between Sarah Miles and Maurice Bendrix, the last “Catholic novel” by Graham Greene.

packet.name: 06 - McCullar et al, Du et al

tossups.question: This novel ends with the protagonist begging God: “I’m too tired and old to learn to love, leave me alone forever.” A boy’s sudden recovery from appendicitis and the clearing of a strawberry birthmark from a preacher’s face are miracles posthumously attributed to a woman in this novel. The protagonist of this novel leaves a funeral with Waverly’s girlfriend and hires the detective Alfred Parkis, who recovers a woman’s diary. The title event of this novel is precipitated by a (*) V-1 rocket exploding during an air raid in front of an apartment where a couple has just had sex. In this novel, the wife of the civil servant Henry flees from the protagonist into a rainy night, catching the pneumonia that kills her. For 10 points, name this novel in which Sarah Miles breaks off her liaison with Maurice Bendrix, by Graham Greene.
tossups.question_sanitized: This novel ends with the protagonist begging God: "I'm too tired and old to learn to love, leave me alone forever." In this novel, the wife of the civil servant Henry flees from the protagonist into a rainy night, catching the pneumonia that kills her. For 10 points, name this novel in which Sarah Miles breaks off her liaison with Maurice Bendrix, by Graham Greene.

tossups.question: Two characters in this novel indicate their interest in each other by dining on steak and onions at Rules. Near the end of this novel, an ugly strawberry mark miraculously disappears from the face of Richard Smythe. Inspired by its author’s relationship with Lady Catherine Walston, this novel is narrated by a man who hires the inept detective Mr. Parkis to steal a diary, and later arranges the cremation of his former lover out of spite. The climactic moment of this novel occurs when a woman promises to leave her lover if God returns him to life during an air raid. Set in London during World War Two, it focuses on the relationship between a novelist and the wife of the English statesman Henry. For 10 points, name this 1951 novel about Maurice Bendix’s illicit romance with Sarah Miles, written by Graham Greene.

packet.name: J (South Carolina A, Cambridge A, GT A, Maryland B, UNC C)

bonuses.leadin: A woman in this novel desperately prays to God to save the protagonist from the explosion of a V-1 rocket that landed near him. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel by Graham Greene in which the adulterous Sarah Miles converts to Catholicism after Maurice Bendrix survives a bombing during the Blitz.
The Blitz also provides the backdrop for a novel by this author that follows the counterspy Harrison and Stella Rodney, who is in a relationship with the Nazi spy Robert Kelway.
The Pevensie children evacuate London during the Blitz in this author’s novel The Lion, the Witch, and the Wardrobe.
answers: Elizabeth Bowen [or Elizabeth Dorothea Cole Bowen] (The novel is The Heat of the Day.)
answers_sanitized: Elizabeth Bowen [or Elizabeth Dorothea Cole Bowen] (The novel is The Heat of the Day.)
C. S. Lewis [or Clive Staples Lewis]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A woman in this novel desperately prays to God to save the protagonist from the explosion of a V-1 rocket that landed near him. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this novel by Graham Greene in which the adulterous Sarah Miles converts to Catholicism after Maurice Bendrix survives a bombing during the Blitz.
The Blitz also provides the backdrop for a novel by this author that follows the counterspy Harrison and Stella Rodney, who is in a relationship with the Nazi spy Robert Kelway.
The Pevensie children evacuate London during the Blitz in this author's novel The Lion, the Witch, and the Wardrobe.

bonuses.leadin: A character in this novel dies of a lung infection worsened by a walk across Clapham Common on a rainy day. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel, the last of one author’s ‘Catholic novels’. In this novel, the protagonist, based on the author, has a relationship with Sarah Miles, the wife of an impotent civil servant.
Sarah Miles breaks off her affair with Maurice Bendrix after his house is destroyed by a bomb during this event. The Ministry of Fear is also set during this bombing campaign.
In one novel from this non-Greene series of novels, Lady Molly dies during the Blitz, which Nick Jenkins discovers because he is about to inform her of a bombing at the Café de Madrid.
answers: A Dance to the Music of Time
answers_sanitized: A Dance to the Music of Time

bonuses.leadin: In Graham Greene’s The Ministry of Fear, Arthur Rowe is given a cake containing some secret film just before his house is destroyed in this event. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this historical event. The Pevensie children are evacuated from the city to escape this event in C. S. Lewis’s The Lion, the Witch, and the Wardrobe. Description acceptable.
In this Graham Greene short story, the only house in a neighborhood not damaged by the Blitz is demolished by the teenage “Wormsley Common gang.”
In this novel by Greene, Sarah Miles promises God that she will stop committing adultery with Maurice Bendrix if Maurice revives after apparently dying during a Blitz bombing.
answers: the Blitz [or German bombing of London during World War II; or air raids on London; prompt on “bombing of England,” “Battle of Britain,” “World War II,” or similar]
answers_sanitized: the Blitz [or German bombing of London during World War II; or air raids on London; prompt on "bombing of England," "Battle of Britain," "World War II," or similar]

parts_sanitized: Identify this historical event. The Pevensie children are evacuated from the city to escape this event in C. S. Lewis's The Lion, the Witch, and the Wardrobe. Description acceptable.
In this Graham Greene short story, the only house in a neighborhood not damaged by the Blitz is demolished by the teenage "Wormsley Common gang."
In this novel by Greene, Sarah Miles promises God that she will stop committing adultery with Maurice Bendrix if Maurice revives after apparently dying during a Blitz bombing.

bonuses.leadin: In this novel, the private investigator Alfred Parkis discovers that Sarah Miles has been meeting Richard Smythe, giving this information to one of Sarah's ex-lovers. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which Maurice Bendix survives a bomb blast in his building, prompting Sarah to effect the title event as part of a promise to God.
This character created by the author of The End of the Affair is invited to Mr. Tench's home while he waits for a boat to Veracruz. He has an illegitimate daughter whom he searches for in Mexico.
This English novelist wrote The End of the Affair and about the Whiskey Priest in The Power and the Glory.

bonuses.leadin: The narrator describes this novel as “a record of hate far more than of love” in its opening passages, shortly after declaring “A story has no beginning or end”. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this novel in which the writer Maurice Bendrix cuckolds his neighbor Henry Miles until a bomb blast causes Sarah, Bendrix’s lover and Henry’s wife, to adopt religion and reconsider her life.
The End of the Affair was written by this Catholic convert, a British novelist who also wrote The Power and the Glory, Our Man in Havana, and The Quiet American.
In this Greene novel, cafe owner Ida Arnold investigates the murder of Fred Hale, a newspaper reporter, by the sociopathic gang leader Pinkie Brown.

bonuses.leadin: After enjoying a few days of safety in the house of Mr. Lehr, this character crosses the border again to hear a dying man's confession. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this character who evades the Lieutenant in his attempt to leave his home state, where his religion has been outlawed. On his way, he meets the initially untrustworthy mestizo.
The whiskey priest is the main character of this British author's novel The Power and the Glory.
This partly-autobiographical Greene novel traces the relationship between Sarah Miles, the wife of an impotent government worker, and the writer Maurice Bendrix.
